other_observers: Nathan Pieplow; first found and identified by myself
description: the bird was a fairly small flycatcher, the shape and size as a Say's Phoebe. The posture of the bird was upright, like a flycatcher, and not horizontal. The tail was fairly long, and the bill was short and thin. The bill and legs were black.  The plumage of the bird was entirely black, except for a white belly.Behaviors: The bird was seen on shrubs along a large river, flycatcher over the river and occasionally landing on rocks in the river.Call: none heardPlumage: adult
